claims.statement: On this machine and the recorded one-million-cell numeric level-6 targeted-upsert workload, the isolated target-entry residual median fell from 131892 to 603 microseconds after the normal path reused staged file CRC32 metadata; median staged-CRC validation was 152 microseconds and process peak working set remained about 8.34 MB.

The optimized one-million-cell formula workload with an external hyperlink reported a 562-microsecond target-entry residual median, a 156-microsecond staged-CRC validation median and an 8.33594 MB process peak working set median; the representative output opened with openpyxl 3.1.2 and preserved the recorded hyperlink target.

Focused tests preserved chunk-specific stale-file diagnostics, failure-before-state-change, output protection and retry. Production and stored-only CTest passed 132/132, and no-images plus all three install-consumer profiles passed.

The observed target-entry total median changed from 2330860 to 1669776 microseconds, but minizip write and machine scheduling varied; this evidence does not generalize the observation into a cross-machine, arbitrary-workbook, Office or full-feature throughput claim.
